directions:

directions:

title: Cracked Wheat & Graham Bread
ingredients:
1 pkg dry yeast
2½ C warm water (about 110 deg)
1 t sugar
2 C graham flour
½ C cracked wheat
1 C warm water (about 110 deg)
2 T salad oil
1 T salt
¼ C brown sugar
1 C each of wheat germ and rolled oats
2 T sesame seed
5 to 5½ C all-purpose flour
Directions
directions:
In a large bowl, dissolve yeast in the 2½ C water. Add sugar, graham flour and cracked wheat. Beat until well combined (about 3 min). Let stand in a warm place until bubbly (about 20 min).
In the 1 C water stir oil, salt, brown sugar, wheat germ, rolled oats and sesame seed. Beat in about 4 C of the all-purpose flour to make a stiff dough. Turn dough out onto a floured board; knead until smooth and satiny (5 to 20 min), adding flour as needed to prevent sticking.
Turn dough over in a greased bowl; cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled (about 1½ hours). Punch dough down; knead briefly on a lightly floured board to release air. Divide dough into four equal portions, shape each into a ball, and place two balls side by side in each of two greased, 9x5 inch, loaf pans. Cover and let rise until almost doubled (about 35 min).
Bake in a 375 deg oven for 45 min or until loaves sound hollow when tapped. Cool in pans for 10 min, then turn out on a rack to cool completely. Makes two loaves.

title: Portuguese Sweet Bread (Pão Doce)
ingredients:
¾ cup instant mashed potato granules or powder
⅔ cup boiling water
⅔ cup sugar
½ cup instant non-fat dry milk
½ cup butter or margarine
2 teaspoons salt
½ teaspoon lemon flavoring
1 egg, beaten
Granulated sugar (optional)
Directions
directions:
In a small pan, beat instant potatoes into the boiling water. Stir in the ⅔ cup sugar, dry milk, and butter. Let cool to 110°.
Meanwhile, in the large bowl of an electric mixer, dissolve yeast in the warm water. Blend in cooled potato mixture.
Add 2 cups of the flour and beat to blend. Stir in eggs, salt, and lemon flavoring until smoothly blended.
Then beat in another 1½ cups flour.
With a heavy mixer or by hand, mix in more flour to make a stiff dough, 1 to 1½ cups. Turn dough out onto a floured board; knead until smooth and satiny (5 to 20 minutes until smooth and satiny (5 to 20 minutes), adding flour as needed to prevent sticking.
Turn dough over in a greased bowl; cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
Punch dough down and knead briefly on a lightly floured board to release air. Let rest 10 minutes. Shape in one of the following ways:
Coiled loaves:
Divide dough in half. Roll each piece into a 30-inch-long rope. For each loaf, coil one rope into a greased 9-inch pie pan, starting at the outside edge of pan and ending in the center. Twist rope slightly as you lay it in the pan. Makes 2 loaves.
Coiled buns:
Divide dough into 12 equal-size pieces. Roll each into a 12-inch-long rope. On greased baking sheets, coil and twist each rope as directed for coiled loaves to make 2½- to 3-inch buns. Space buns at least 2 inches apart. Makes 1 dozen.
Braided loaves:
Divide dough into 6 equal portions and roll each into a 14-inch-long rope. For each loaf, arrange 3 ropes side by side on a greased baking sheet and loosely braid. Pinch both ends together and turn under loaf. Makes 2 loaves.
Round loaves:
Divide dough in half. Shape each half into a flattened round, about 8 inches across, and place in a greased 9-inch pie pan. Makes 2 loaves.
Cover loaves or buns lightly and let rise in a warm place until almost doubled, 40 to 45 minutes for loaves, 20 to 30 minutes for buns. Brush with beaten egg; sprinkle with granulated sugar, if desired. Bake in a 350° oven until browned, 25 to 30 minutes for loaves, 20 to 25 minutes for buns. Cool on racks. Serve warm or cooled.
To freeze, cool completely, wrap tightly in foil, and place in freezer. To reheat, unwrap and let thaw at room temperature. Place on a baking sheet and loosely cover with foil. Bake in a 350° oven until warm, 10 to 15 minutes for buns, 25 to 30 minutes for loaves.

title: Old Lemon Soufflé with Wine Sauce
ingredients:
Souffle
Envelope Knox gelatine
¼ cup cold water
5 eggs separated
¾ cup fresh lemon juice
3 teaspoons grated lemon rind
1½ cups sugar
1 cup heavy cream
Wine Sauce
⅔ cup sugar
1 tablespoon cornstarch
½ cup water
3 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
1 teaspoon grated lemon rind
2 tablespoons butter
½ cup dry white wine
Directions
Souffle
directions:
Soften gelatine by sprinkling over cold water. Mix egg yolks with lemon juice, rind, and ¾ cup sugar.
Put in top of double boiler and cook, stirring constantly, until lemon mixture is slightly thickened (about 5 minutes, if over boiling water).
Remove from heat and stir in gelatine until dissolved.
Chill 30-40 minutes or until mixture molds slightly when dropped from spoon.
Beat egg whites until they begin to hold shape, then gradually add ¾ cup sugar until all has been added and whites are stiff.
Fold whites and stiffly beaten whipped cream into yolk mixture until all white strips or streaks are gone.
Pour into a 2-quart mold or soufflé dish and chill 6+ hours or more.
Serve with wine sauce.
Wine Sauce
In a pan, mix sugar and cornstarch. Stir in water, lemon juice, and rind until smooth.
Add butter.
Bring to a boil, lower heat, and cook until thick. Stir.
Remove from heat and stir in wine.
Chill, stirring occasionally.

ingredients:

directions:
About 4 hours before serving or day ahead: In 2-quart saucepan over medium heat, heat lingonberries, sugar, and 2½ cups water to boiling. Reduce heat to low and cook 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Press mixture through sieve into medium bowl. Discard pulp; measure juice and add enough water, if necessary, to make 4 cups. Return juice to saucepan; over high heat, heat to boiling. Reduce heat to low; gradually stir in farina and salt; cook until mixture is very thick, about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Pour lingonberry mixture into large bowl, with mixer at high speed, beat until mixture mounds when dropped from spoon, about 15 minutes; cover and refrigerate until firm, about 3 hours. To serve: Spoon pudding into dessert dishes and serve with half-and-half. Makes 12 servings.

Combine tuna and rice in 2 qt. casserole.
Combine soup, water, sour cream, tarragon and 1/2 tsp. of the salt in saucepan. Heat to boiling stirring constantly. Pour over tuna and rice in casserole.
Arrange about 1/2 artichoke hearts in circle petal fashion, in center of casserole. Pile remaining artich. hrts. in center. Sprinkle artich. with remaining 1/2 tsp salt. Cover and bake at 400° for 30 min.
To serve, sprinkle with parsley and put pimiento in center of artich.

ingredients:

Grill or broil chicken for 2 min on each side. Remove to platter, sprinkle remaining cilantro + serve with sautéed peppers.

title: Saged Turkey Loaf
ingredients:
4 slices firm-textured bread, trimmed of crusts and cubed
3/4 cup chicken broth
1 teaspoon vegetable oil
1/2 cup finely chopped shallots
1/2 cup finely chopped celery
1 1/2 teaspoons dried sage leaves, crushed
1 tablespoon minced fresh sage
1/2 teaspoon dried marjoram, crushed
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon black pepper
1/2 teaspoon white pepper
1/2 teaspoon dried orange peel
2 pounds ground raw turkey
2 egg whites, lightly beaten
8 fresh sage leaves for garnish
directions:
Place the bread and chicken broth in a small bowl and set aside.
Heat the oil in a sauté pan over medium heat, add the shallots and the celery; cook and stir 3 minutes. Add the dried sage, fresh sage, thyme, marjoram, salt, black pepper, white pepper and orange peel. Cook and stir 1 minute.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the bread, shallot mixture, turkey and egg whites. Mix well and pack into a lightly greased 9-by-5-inch loaf pan; arrange fresh sage leaves on top. Bake in a preheated 350-degree oven for 1 hour 20 minutes, or until an internal temperature registers 170 degrees. Let sit 10 minutes before slicing.

title: Hot Tomato Starter
ingredients:
1 can condensed tomato soup
1 can condensed mushroom soup
1 can water
¼ teaspoon ground cumin
¼ teaspoon cayenne
¼ teaspoon thyme
Butter
Top Ingredients:
1 can corn (or whole kernel)
1¼ #2 pkg frozen corn
1 can mushroom soup (or Campbell mushroom soup)
1 can french fried onion rings
Top Ingredients:
directions:
Combine corn and mushroom soup.
Mix with onion mixture.
Top with french fried onion rings.
Directions
Combine tomato soup and water in a saucepan. Mix well.
Add condensed mushroom soup to the mixture.
Stir in the cumin, cayenne, and thyme.
Heat the mixture, stirring occasionally, until it reaches your desired temperature.
Serve hot, garnished with butter if desired.

title: Kase Knepfle
ingredients:
Cottage cheese
Eggs
Salt & Peppers
Green onions (onion tops or chives)
Dough:
2-2½ cups flour (salt to taste)
Water to make stiff dough
directions:
With a fork or pastry blender cottage cheese, add eggs, salt, pepper, and green onions thoroughly.
For dough: Mix flour with water and 1 egg white to make stiff dough. Knead and let set about ½ hour (covered).
Roll out like cookie dough and cut into 2" squares.
Lift the square with the underside up, place filling on dough pieces, fold over in a diamond shape diagonally, and pinch sides together securely.
Drop into salted boiling water and cook for 20 minutes (simmering).
Lift each one from hot water with a slotted spoon and drain a bit.
Bown knepfle in brown butter and add a few dry bread crumbs.
Serve with sour cream to which a little of the cheese and onion mixture has been added.

title: Tomatos Farcies
ingredients:
12 tomatoes
1 onion, cut up
6 cloves of garlic
Parsley, ground
500 g ground beef
400 g "chair à saucisse" (sausage meat) or any leftover meat
2 tablespoons butter or oil
4 egg yolks
Bread or finely crushed biscotti (optional)
2½ glasses of rice or whatever you wish to eat around the tomatoes
½ teaspoon butter for each tomato top
1 cup bouillon
Salt
Directions
directions:
Empty the tomatoes by cutting off the ends without the stem. Salt them and place them in a pan (about 12 tomatoes for this recipe).
Cut up 1 onion and 6 cloves of garlic. Grind the parsley.
Mix 500 g ground beef and 400 g "chair à saucisse" (you can use any leftover meat for this dish).
Add 2 tablespoons butter or oil in a frying pan and fry the meat. Add onions, parsley, and garlic.
Optional: You can add some bread or finely crushed biscotti in the meat mixture.
Add 4 egg yolks and cook. Cool the mixture.
Stuff the tomatoes to their tops.
Put 2½ glasses of rice (or whatever you wish to eat around the tomatoes) in the pan.
Place the tomato lids on top with a piece of butter on them (½ teaspoon each).
Put salt over everything.
Take the tomatoes' insides (cook them if you want) and pour it on the rice. Add 1 cup bouillon.
Cook for 30 minutes until the rice is done. As soon as it is, everything else is ready.
Hint: If it becomes dry in the oven, add more bouillon.

title: Barbecue Sauce
ingredients:
½ lb butter
1 clove garlic, cut fine
¼ cup soy sauce
1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce
2 tablespoons? mustard
1 tablespoon ketchup
directions: Combine all Ingredients: and heat until ready to serve hot.

title: Chicken Soup a la Frecque
ingredients:
1 heavy stewing hen (6 lbs +)
1 large carrot, coarsely diced
1 large onion, skin on, stuck with cloves
2 sticks celery with tops (cubed or chopped)
1 bay leaf
Parsley
½ teaspoon dried thyme (2 cans condensed broth)
2 cups dry white wine (½ long or rice)
4 eggs
Juice of 2-3 lemons
½ cup heavy cream
3 onions, chopped finely
Directions
In a casserole, place the heavy stewing hen (about 6 lbs or more).
directions:
Add the large carrot (coarsely diced), 1 large onion with skin on stuck with cloves, 2 sticks of celery with tops (cubed or chopped), 1 bay leaf, parsley, and ½ teaspoon dried thyme (or 2 cans condensed broth).
Add 2 cups dry white wine (or ½ long rice if preferred).
Pour in water or broth to cover everything, and pour it in the casserole to cover.
Return the chicken and add cream to set the sauce.
Finely chop 3 onions and add to the mixture.
Whip 4 eggs with the juice of 2-3 lemons. Add this mixture with ½ tablespoon of hot broth.
Pour the egg mixture into the casserole while stirring constantly to avoid curdling.
Continue stirring and cook until the soup is well blended and heated through.
Serve with parsley garnish.

title: Chicken Breast Cutlets
ingredients:
4 whole chicken breasts, split, boned and skinned
3/4 cup fine dry bread crumbs
1/3 cup grated Parmesan cheese
Salad oil
Paprika, salt and pepper
Lemon-mushroom Sauce (recipe follows)
directions:
Place each half chicken breast between 2 pieces plastic wrap.
Pound with mallet (or potato masher) until meat is about 3/4" thick.
Combine bread crumbs and cheese in shallow container.
Lightly brush both sides of meat with oil.
Dip into crumb mixture to coat evenly.
Bake in single layer in greased 10x15 pan.
Sprinkle with paprika, salt, and pepper.
Bake 15 min. or until meat is no longer pink when slashed.

title: Lemon-Mushroom Sauce
ingredients:
3 tablespoons butter
¼ pound mushrooms, sliced
3 tablespoons lemon juice
1 tablespoon chopped parsley
¼ teaspoon dry rosemary
directions:
Melt butter.
Add mushrooms and cook until golden.
Add lemon juice, chopped parsley, and dry rosemary.
Heat to simmering.
Pour over chicken cutlets arranged on platter or individual servings.

title: Chicken Curry
ingredients:
Chicken
2 whole chickens (about 3 lbs each)
Water
1 tablespoon salt
1 large onion, cut in eighths
1 carrot, sliced
2 stalks celery, cut up
6 whole black peppercorns
Bouquet garni (2 sprigs parsley, 1 bay leaf, 1 tsp thyme)
Curry Sauce:
3 tablespoons butter
1 clove garlic, crushed
1 cup chopped onion
2-3 teaspoons curry powder
1 cup pared chopped apple
¼ cup unsifted all-purpose flour
½ teaspoon ground cardamom
1 teaspoon ginger
1 teaspoon salt
¼ teaspoon pepper
2 teaspoons grated lime peel
2 tablespoons fresh lime juice
directions:
Place chickens in large pot and cover with water.
Add salt, onion, carrot, celery, peppercorns, and bouquet garni.
Bring to rolling boil. Skim; reduce heat to simmer.
Cover and cook 1 to 1¼ hours.
Turn off heat and let stand until cool.
Remove chickens and take meat from bones in large pieces.
Strain liquid and remove fat; reserve 3 cups.
For sauce: Melt butter in large skillet.
Sauté garlic, onion, curry powder, and apple until onion is tender (about 5 minutes).
Remove from heat and stir in flour, cardamom, ginger, salt, and pepper.
Gradually stir in reserved chicken stock.
Add lime peel and lime juice.
Bring to boil, stirring. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, 20 minutes.
Stir in cooked chicken and heat to boiling point.
Serve with rice and condiments: chopped green pepper, chutney, whole salted peanuts, flaked coconut, mandarin oranges, raisins, pineapple chunks, and sliced green onions.

title: Champignons à la Crème (Creamed Mushrooms)
ingredients:
Mushrooms
Butter
Parsley
Garlic
Salt and pepper
Flour
½ cup cream
directions:
Put the mushrooms on low heat with nothing added (so the water in the mushrooms will evaporate).
Then put in a good amount of butter and stir.
Add parsley, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Then add a bit of flour.
After that is all mixed in, add about half a cup of cream.
Stir in and presto, there it is!

title: Gourmet Spiced Beef with Peas
ingredients:
¾ cup finely chopped onion
2 tablespoons clarified butter
1½ teaspoons each turmeric and coriander
1 teaspoon cayenne salt
⅛ teaspoon hot red pepper
1 pound chopped beef
1 cup peeled, seeded, chopped tomatoes
1 cup fresh green peas
directions:
Sauté onion in clarified butter until tender.
Stir in turmeric, coriander, cayenne salt, and hot red pepper. Cook, stirring, for 5 minutes.
Add chopped beef and brown.
Stir in tomatoes, cover, and simmer 20 minutes.
Stir in fresh green peas and cook another 15 minutes.
Serve with rice.

title: Swiss Potato Soup (Erdöpfel Suppe)
ingredients:
4 large white potatoes
3 cups water
1 teaspoon salt
¼ teaspoon marjoram
2 tablespoons chopped celery leaves (tender inner leaves only)
5 green onions
2 tablespoons butter
2 tablespoons flour
1 cup milk or half-and-half
½ cup finely chopped parsley
¼ teaspoon pepper
About 1 cup shredded Swiss cheese
directions:
Peel potatoes and slice (about 5 cups).
Place in a large saucepan and add water, salt, marjoram, celery leaves, and the white part only of 3 onions, finely chopped.
Bring to a boil and simmer 25 minutes, or until very tender.
Remove from heat and mash with a potato masher.
Melt butter and blend in flour; cook 2 minutes, stirring.
Gradually pour in milk and cook until thickened, stirring constantly.
Add to potato mixture.
Finely chop remaining 2 onions (using both white and green parts) and add to soup along with parsley and pepper.
Cover and let stand a few minutes.
Pass cheese. Serves 8 with 1 cup each.
notes:
Note: It's earth apple soup. It's Swiss
Serves: 8

title: Scandinavian Fish Cakes
ingredients:
1 cup fine white cod
⅓ cup milk

½ teaspoon salt

Ground pepper
directions:

Form into cakes approximately 2 inches, dropped from spoon.
Brown in ½ butter and ½ Mazola oil mixture.
Serve with lemon seasoning.
Garnish with small boiled potatoes, carrots, and parsley.

Salt
directions: Mix all ingredients together and serve warm over fish.

Add tomato sauce, lentils, cold water, and ham hocks.

During last hour of cooking, add vinegar, Tabasco, garlic powder, Worcestershire, and ginger.
May be thickened with flour and water if desired.
Serve with broth.

title: Aubergine Hot Dish (Eggplant)
ingredients:

Eggs
Oil for frying

Cheese
directions:

Place fried eggplant on bottom of dish.
Put layer of meat (½ pork/½ hamburger).
Then cover with aubergine (eggplant).
Cover with cheese.
Bake until done.

title: Cabbage Rolls
ingredients:
2 (8 oz) cans tomato sauce
¼ cup firmly packed brown sugar
¼ cup vinegar
Chili powder
1 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce
2 teaspoons salt
1 teaspoon dry mustard
Filling Ingredients:
3 large cabbage leaves
1 egg
1 pound lean ground beef
¾ cup well-drained rice
½ cup evaporated milk
¼ cup finely cut onions
1 teaspoon salt
2 teaspoons Worcestershire sauce
¼ teaspoon pepper
¼ cup Parmesan cheese
directions:
Cook cabbage leaves in boiling water in a covered 4-quart saucepan until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain well.
Mix sauce ingredients in small bowl and let it sit.
Mix stuffing ingredients in a 3-quart bowl. Put about ⅓ cup of stuffing in center of cabbage leaves. Fold leaves over mixture. Secure with wooden picks.
Pour 1 cup of tomato mixture into greased shallow 2-quart baking dish. Place rolls into sauce in dish. Pour remaining sauce over rolls.
Bake at 350° about 50 minutes. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese before serving.

title: Cauliflower Casserole
ingredients:
Cauliflower
Basil/spices
Garlic, salt
Lemon, sugar
Potatoes, cut long and narrow
Ground pepper
1 large onion
½ teaspoon turmeric
4 whole cloves
Ginger
Cinnamon stick
3-4 cardamom pods
Butter
2 eggs
Tomatoes, seared
Peas
directions:
Cook cauliflower with basil/spices. Drain and set aside.
Fry potatoes cut long and narrow. Drain on paper.
Fry onion with ½ teaspoon turmeric, ginger, 4 whole cloves, cinnamon stick, 3-4 cardamom, and butter until brown.
Beat 2 eggs and dip cauliflower in egg mixture (optional).
Add seared tomatoes for color on top.
Add peas.
Bake until done.

title: Florentine Gratine Sole
ingredients:
2 (10 oz) packages chopped spinach
2 cups sour cream
3 tablespoons flour
½ cup finely chopped green onions (including some of the green tops)
Juice of 1 fresh lemon
½ teaspoon salt
2 tablespoons butter
1½ - 2 pounds fillet of sole
Paprika
Parsley
directions:
Bring water to a boil and cook spinach 5 minutes. Drain thoroughly.
Mix sour cream with flour, green onions, lemon juice, and salt.
Mix half of the sour cream mixture with the spinach. Save half for the top.
Spread 2 tablespoons butter on the bottom of a shallow baking dish (an oval dish preferred, 15 x 2 x 2 approximately).
Arrange spinach mixture as first layer in dish.
Layer the fish, overlapping when necessary for the second layer.
Spread the remaining 1 cup sour cream over the fish and sprinkle with paprika and parsley.
Bake at 375° for 25 minutes.

title: Fish Cakes
ingredients:
1 cup fish, cut into chunks
1⅛ cups milk
2 tablespoons potato flour
1 teaspoon onion salt
½ teaspoon salt
White pepper
Nutmeg to taste
directions:
Combine all ingredients.
Form into cakes.
Fry until golden brown.

title: Baked Chicken with Tomato
directions:
Arrange chicken on baking sheet.
Salt and pepper them.
Spread tomato mixture around and between pieces (not on top of chicken).
Bake 35-45 minutes, Oven at 400° checking to be sure tomatoes aren't burning.
Add little water if necessary.

title: Baked East Indian Chicken Thighs
ingredients:
1 cup roughly chopped onion
4 cloves garlic
1 (2-inch) piece fresh ginger, peeled and roughly chopped (about ¼ cup)
3 tablespoons fresh lime juice
2 tablespoons vegetable oil
1 teaspoon salt
2 teaspoons ground cumin
1 teaspoon ground coriander
½ teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes
⅛ teaspoon ground cardamom
4 chicken thighs, skin removed and fat trimmed away (about 1½ pounds)
2 tablespoons chopped cilantro (optional)
directions:
In a blender or food processor, combine the onion, garlic, ginger, lime juice, oil, salt, cumin, coriander, pepper flakes, and cardamom. Blend until pureed.
Place the chicken thighs in a shallow baking pan and cover with the purée.
Cover and refrigerate for 2 to 24 hours.
Preheat oven to 450 degrees. Line a rimmed baking sheet with foil and spray with nonstick cooking spray.
Carefully arrange the chicken on the prepared pan to keep as much of the purée on the chicken pieces as possible.
Bake for 25 minutes or until an instant-read thermometer placed in the thickest part of the largest thigh registers 165 degrees.
Remove from oven and sprinkle with cilantro, if using.
Serve immediately.
Per Serving: Calories 252 (45% from fat); Protein 26.6g; Total fat 12.5g; Saturated fat 1.84g; Cholesterol 108.2mg; Sodium 698mg; Carbohydrates 7.9g; Dietary fiber 1.1g

title: Norwegian Fish Pudding (Fiske Grot)
ingredients:
½ cup butter or margarine
3 big tablespoons flour
2 cups milk
1 pound white fish (no bones)
¼ teaspoon nutmeg
¼ teaspoon pepper
½ teaspoon salt
4 eggs
Buttered bread crumbs for topping
directions:
Melt butter and add flour until smooth.
Add fish and seasoning.
Add milk and bring to boil.
Put buttered bread crumbs on top.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Cover top if too brown.

title: Grape Leaf Rolls

Grape leaves
Cooked sauce (4 tomatoes with onion)
½ pork and ½ beef, raw
1 cup uncooked rice
Lemon juice
Mint leaves (4 tablespoons or more)
Pepper and salt
directions:
Mix meat with rice, lemon juice, mint leaves, pepper, and salt.
Place filling in grape leaves and roll.
Arrange in pan with tomato sauce.
Cook until done.

title: Meat and Carrots
ingredients:
Veal, cut in chunks (lean)
Thyme
Laurier (bay leaf)
Oregano
Onions
Carrots, sliced
directions:

title: Florentine Sole

Sour cream mixture
2 cups sour cream mixed with 3 tablespoons flour
½ cup finely chopped green onions (with some green tops)
Juice of one lemon
2 teaspoons salt
2 tablespoons butter
1½-2 lbs fillets of sole
directions:

Spread 2 tablespoons butter on bottom of shallow baking dish (15" x 8½" approximate).
Arrange spinach mixture as first layer in pan.

Spread the 1 cup remaining sour cream over the fish and sprinkle with paprika & parsley
Do not overcook – 375 degrees for 25 minutes

title: Ribs with BBQ Sauce
contributor: Charlotte Jack Robins - friend of BBQ Beef
ingredients:
3 tablespoons sugar
1 (14 oz) bottle catsup
½ cup water

directions:

Peroshkee (Meat-Filled Pastries)
Soften yeast in lukewarm water.
Scald milk. Add sugar, salt, shortening. Cool to lukewarm.
Add 1 cup flour. Add softened yeast and eggs.
Add flour to make soft dough. Turn on floured board and knead about 10 minutes.
Place in greased bowl. Cover and let raise till double in bulk.
Punch down, cover and let rest 10 minutes.
Form into balls the size of a small egg. Flatten into rounds.
Place heaping spoonful of filling into center.
Bring up sides and pinch together securely.
Finished peroshkee resemble little footballs.
Fry seam down in deep fryer at 350-370 degrees. Turn once.
Filling:
Fry meat, breaking up and stirring constantly till browned.
Sautee onions in butter.
Chop eggs fine.
Mix all Ingredients: and cool.
Pelemeni
(Russian Dumplings)
Makes approximately 220 dumplings
Make the dough: Mix 2 eggs with 2 cups water and salt.
Gradually add approximately 3 lbs of flour to create a firm dough.
Knead the dough until smooth and elastic. Let it rest.
Prepare the filling: Grind or finely chop 1-2 or 3 lbs of pork and/or beef.
Finely chop 1 onion and add to the meat.
Add 5 eggs to the meat mixture (or use ⅓ cup egg/3 eggs plus ⅓ water for a lighter filling).
Mix the filling thoroughly and season with salt to taste.
Roll out the dough thinly on a floured surface.
Cut circles from the dough (about 2-3 inches in diameter).
Place a small amount of filling in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over to create a half-moon shape and pinch the edges firmly to seal.
Bring the two corners together to form the classic pelemeni shape.
Boil the pelemeni in salted water until they float to the surface (about 5-7 minutes).
Serve hot with sour cream, butter, or vinegar.
Note: Pelemeni can be frozen before cooking. Place them on a tray, freeze until solid, then store in freezer bags. Cook directly from frozen, adding a few extra minutes to the cooking time.Приятного аппетита! (Enjoy your meal!)

directions:
In about a 1-quart pan, heat the milk and butter to lukewarm (butter need not melt completely); set aside. In a large bowl, mix yeast with water. Let stand 5 minutes to soften. Blend in milk mixture, grated peel, salt, sugar, whole egg, and egg yolks. Add 1½ cups of the flour and beat with electric mixer on medium speed for 5 minutes, scraping bowl occasionally. Add remaining flour and beat with mixer or by hand until well blended. The dough should be soft but not too sticky when lightly patted. Turn dough over in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place until the dough has more than doubled, about 2 hours.
Punch down the dough, turn it out onto a well-greased 12- by 15-inch baking sheet, and flatten into a 10-inch round. Cover lightly with plastic wrap and let rise at room temperature (place should be not quite as warm as for original rising) until the dough has risen to a height of about 2 inches; allow about 35 minutes.
Bake in a 350° oven until a light golden brown, about 25 to 30 minutes. With a large spatula, slide cake onto a wire rack to cool completely. Place cake on a serving plate or board. With a long serrated knife, cut it horizontally in half. Spread filling over bottom half and replace the top half. Cover lightly with plastic wrap and chill at least 6 hours or overnight.
To serve, scatter crushed sugar cubes on top and press them in lightly; dust with powdered sugar. Makes 12 servings.
